title: triticum repens var. sepium
intro: Triticum repens var. sepium, also known as couch grass or quack grass, is a type of grassy weed that belongs to the wheat family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of couch grass include its ability to stimulate the kidneys and bladder, making it useful in treating issues such as urinary tract infections and kidney stones. The health benefits of couch grass include its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and improve overall urinary function. Couch grass can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to support kidney and urinary health.
